summary: OpenReadOnly checks PRAGMA user_version and returns the upgrade-CLI error when the stored schema exceeds StoreSchemaVersion.
reason: OpenWithContext routed through migrate() which surfaces a friendly 'database schema version N is newer than supported version M; upgrade the CLI binary' error, but OpenReadOnly skipped the check entirely. All six read commands (tail, owner, model, event, search, flight) flow through OpenReadOnly, so a user running `sync` with a newer binary and then a read with an older binary would hit raw SQLite column-binding errors instead of the actionable upgrade message. The patch mirrors migrate()'s version-mismatch error in OpenReadOnly without a write transaction; the StoreSchemaVersion code comment that promises 'checked on every open' is now actually true.
